title: Yara and the Missing Carrots
synopsis: When carrots start disappearing from the garden, young detective Yara teams up with her animal friends to solve the mystery and discover that the thief might not be who they expect.

pages.text: Yara loved her garden. She had planted carrots, tomatoes, and beans. Every morning, she checked on her plants. "Good morning, little carrots," she would say. "Grow big and orange!"

illustration.description: A young Black girl with curly hair in a colorful sundress kneeling in a vibrant garden. She's smiling at rows of green carrot tops growing in rich brown soil. Bright morning sunlight filters through the scene with warm golden tones.
illustration.alt: Yara tending to her garden in the morning sunshine

pages.text: But one Tuesday morning, something was wrong. Three of her biggest carrots were gone! Only holes were left in the dirt. "Oh no!" Yara said. "Someone took my carrots!"

illustration.description: Yara with wide surprised eyes and hands on her cheeks, looking down at three empty holes in the garden soil where carrots used to be. The holes are perfectly round and deep, with loose dirt scattered around them.
illustration.alt: Yara discovers empty holes where her carrots used to grow

pages.text: Yara put on her detective hat. It was purple with a yellow star. "I will find who took my carrots," she said. "This is a mystery to solve!"

illustration.description: Yara wearing a bright purple detective hat with a cheerful yellow star, striking a determined pose with hands on her hips. Her expression is confident and excited, with sparkles around the hat showing her detective spirit.
illustration.alt: Yara wearing her purple detective hat, ready to solve the mystery

pages.text: Yara looked around her garden for clues. She found tiny paw prints in the mud. "Aha!" she said. "The carrot thief has paws!"

illustration.description: Yara crouched down examining small muddy paw prints with a magnifying glass. The paw prints are clearly visible in brown mud, leading away from the garden. Yara's expression shows excitement at finding her first clue.
illustration.alt: Yara examining paw prints in the mud with a magnifying glass

pages.text: Just then, her friend Benny the rabbit hopped over. Benny had white fur and long ears. "Hi Yara! What are you doing?" he asked. Yara showed him the paw prints. "Someone stole my carrots," she said.

illustration.description: A friendly white rabbit with long floppy ears and bright blue eyes hopping toward Yara. The rabbit has an innocent, curious expression. Yara is pointing to the paw prints while Benny looks interested in what she's discovered.
illustration.alt: Benny the white rabbit meeting Yara in the garden

pages.text: "That's terrible!" said Benny. "I love carrots too. They're crunchy and sweet!" Yara looked at Benny's paws. They were the same size as the prints in the mud!

illustration.description: Yara looking suspiciously at Benny's white paws while Benny sits innocently. A thought bubble above Yara's head shows the muddy paw prints matching Benny's paws. Benny looks confused about why Yara is staring at his feet.
illustration.alt: Yara comparing Benny's paws to the mysterious prints

pages.text: Luna the squirrel came running down from her tree. She had gray fur and a big fluffy tail. "What's all the fuss about?" Luna asked. Yara told her about the missing carrots.

illustration.description: A lively gray squirrel with a magnificent fluffy tail scampering down a large oak tree. Luna has bright amber eyes and an energetic expression. The tree has vibrant green leaves and brown bark with interesting textures.
illustration.alt: Luna the gray squirrel coming down from her tree

pages.text: "I saw something last night!" Luna said, twitching her tail. "There was digging in your garden. But it was too dark to see who it was." Yara felt excited. This was another clue!

illustration.description: Luna the squirrel with her tail twitched up in excitement, gesturing with her tiny paws as she speaks. Yara leans in with wide curious eyes, clearly interested in what Luna has to say. The scene shows their animated conversation.
illustration.alt: Luna excitedly telling Yara what she saw

pages.text: Diego the hedgehog rolled up to them. He had brown spikes and kind eyes. "Did someone say mystery?" Diego asked. "I love mysteries!" Now Yara had a whole team to help her.

illustration.description: A cute brown hedgehog with gentle dark eyes and spiky quills rolling into a ball and then unrolling to join the group. Diego has a friendly smile and eager expression. The three friends are now gathered together in the garden.
illustration.alt: Diego the hedgehog joining Yara's detective team

pages.text: "We need to catch the carrot thief!" Yara said. "Let's make a trap." They found a big box and some string. Yara put her last carrot under the box as bait.

illustration.description: The four friends working together to set up a cardboard box trap. Yara is placing a bright orange carrot under the propped-up box while Benny, Luna, and Diego help with the string. Everyone looks focused and determined.
illustration.alt: The friends setting up a trap with a box and carrot

pages.text: That night, they hid behind the fence to watch. The moon was bright and full. "Shh," whispered Yara. "Here comes someone!" They saw a small shadow moving toward the trap.

illustration.description: The four friends crouched behind a wooden fence in moonlight, all with wide eyes watching their trap. The full moon casts silver light over the garden, creating dramatic shadows. A mysterious small silhouette approaches the box trap.
illustration.alt: Friends hiding and watching their trap in the moonlight

pages.text: THUMP! The box fell down. "We got them!" Yara cheered. But when they lifted the box, they found the biggest surprise ever. It was Mama Rabbit with three tiny baby bunnies!

illustration.description: A surprised-looking brown mother rabbit sitting with three adorable tiny baby bunnies huddled close to her under the fallen cardboard box. The babies have different colored fur - white, gray, and light brown. All have innocent, wide eyes looking up at their captors.
illustration.alt: Mama Rabbit and her three baby bunnies caught in the trap

pages.text: "Please don't be mad," said Mama Rabbit. "My babies were so hungry. I took your carrots to feed them. I'm sorry." The tiny bunnies looked up with big, sad eyes.

illustration.description: Mama Rabbit looking apologetic with her ears drooped down, while three tiny baby bunnies with enormous innocent eyes peek out from behind her. The scene has warm, gentle lighting that emphasizes the family's vulnerability and need.
illustration.alt: Mama Rabbit apologizing while baby bunnies hide behind her

pages.text: Yara's heart melted. "Oh, you poor family!" she said. "You don't need to steal. I can share!" Benny, Luna, and Diego all nodded. "We can all help!" said Diego.

illustration.description: Yara kneeling down with a warm, compassionate smile, reaching out gently toward the rabbit family. Her friends Benny, Luna, and Diego surround them in a circle, all showing kind and caring expressions. The scene glows with warmth and friendship.
illustration.alt: Yara and friends showing kindness to the rabbit family

pages.text: From that day on, Yara planted extra carrots to share. The baby bunnies grew big and strong. And Yara learned that the best mysteries have happy endings for everyone. "Mystery solved!" she said with a smile.

illustration.description: A beautiful garden scene showing Yara and all her animal friends - Benny, Luna, Diego, Mama Rabbit, and three now-bigger baby bunnies - all sharing a feast of carrots together. The garden is lush and full of vegetables, with everyone looking happy and well-fed.
illustration.alt: Everyone sharing carrots together in the thriving garden

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What did Yara find missing from her garden?
questions.explanation: Yara discovered that three of her biggest carrots were missing, with only holes left in the dirt.

questions.question: What clue did Yara find in the mud?
questions.explanation: Yara found tiny paw prints in the mud, which was her first clue about who might have taken the carrots.

questions.question: Who really took the carrots?
questions.explanation: Mama Rabbit took the carrots to feed her three hungry baby bunnies.

questions.question: How did the friends catch the carrot thief?
questions.explanation: They set up a box trap using string and put Yara's last carrot as bait underneath.

questions.question: How did the story end?
choices: Yara decided to share her carrots
questions.explanation: Yara decided to plant extra carrots to share with the rabbit family, showing kindness and compassion.

sentences.text: Yara put on her detective ____. It was purple with a yellow star.
She found tiny ____ prints in the mud.
Benny the ____ hopped over to help.

pages.instructions: Choose the correct word to complete each sentence from the story.

sentences.text: They made a ____ to catch the carrot thief.
Under the box, they found Mama Rabbit with three tiny ____.
Yara decided to ____ her carrots with the hungry family.
